beats.title: Surveying the fort
beats.boundaryReason: The beat covers the recap, the campsite investigation, and the outer-fort survey before the lurking creature reveals itself.
dateEvidence: session.yaml sets the session date to 1748-03-25
the party is continuing the same fort investigation from earlier that day

beats.title: Ankheg ambush
beats.boundaryReason: The quiet reconnaissance turns into a self-contained combat with the burrowing predator and ends once it is killed.
dateEvidence: the combat follows directly from the same fort approach
no rest or day change occurs during the fight

beats.title: Temple and respite
beats.boundaryReason: After the ankheg fight, the party inspects the ruined headquarters and temple, finds Aagir's everlight, and takes a short rest.
dateEvidence: the party treats the temple stop as a same-day break after combat
the discussion still refers to it as early afternoon

beats.title: Towers and old horn
beats.boundaryReason: The party leaves the temple to search the towers, recovering the scout's magical horn before committing to the cave below.
dateEvidence: the tower search follows immediately after the short rest
the group is still discussing the same afternoon's exploration

beats.title: Statue and floating disk
beats.boundaryReason: The cave descent leads to the petrified explorer, clues about recent intrusion, and the floating-disc coin, ending once the party reforms its marching order.
dateEvidence: the tunnel descent and first cavern exploration are continuous
no meal, camp, or overnight transition interrupts the delve

beats.title: Cockatrice nursery
beats.boundaryReason: The party scouts the cockatrice hatchery, debates the danger, then circles through the cistern cavern toward the deeper source instead of fighting there.
dateEvidence: the group explicitly treats this as a brief scout before pressing on
the same descent continues without a rest or sunset break

beats.title: Skeletons in the dark
beats.boundaryReason: A new cavern opens into an immediate skeleton fight, which ends as the deeper threat steps into view.
dateEvidence: the party is still in the same cave expedition
the skeleton attack is an uninterrupted continuation of the same delve

beats.title: The black staff
beats.boundaryReason: The undead hobgoblin and its obsidian-topped staff take over the scene, and the beat ends once the creature falls and the staff is left behind.
dateEvidence: the staff-bearer enters the same combat immediately after the skeletons
the encounter resolves before the party shifts into analysis and salvage

beats.title: Reading the relic
beats.boundaryReason: With the undead destroyed, the party studies the obsidian staff, identifies the flaw in the crystal, and debates whether to move it or break it in place.
dateEvidence: the party pauses after combat to inspect the artifact
the conversation treats the delve as still part of the same afternoon

beats.title: Shattering the obsidian
beats.boundaryReason: The group strikes the flaw, destroys the obsidian crystal, watches the fort calm, and settles on resting in the temple before the session closes out.
dateEvidence: the party decides to camp in the temple after destroying the crystal
the wrap-up includes the fort feeling calm through the night and into the next morning
